compiled_state.title: ARTIFACTS_DIR mismatch: reviewer sub-agent writes reviewer-findings.json to its own ARTIFACTS_DIR hash, not the target worktree's
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description: When a code-reviewer sub-agent is dispatched and it writes reviewer-findings.json, it uses get_artifacts_dir() which hashes its own REPO_ROOT (the sub-agent's working directory). The orchestrator's record-review.sh reads from the orchestrator's ARTIFACTS_DIR (different hash). This means the file is never found unless the orchestrator manually copies it between directories. Required workaround every review cycle during epic 615f-fad3: compute sha256 of reviewer REPO_ROOT to find /tmp/workflow-plugin-<hash>/, copy reviewer-findings.json to the target worktree's /tmp/workflow-plugin-<target-hash>/. Fix: record-review.sh should accept an explicit --findings-file path argument, OR reviewer sub-agents should be dispatched with WORKFLOW_PLUGIN_ARTIFACTS_DIR set to the orchestrator's ARTIFACTS_DIR.

comments.body:
Classification: behavioral, Score: 2 (BASIC tier)

Root cause candidate: code-review-dispatch.md prompt template does NOT instruct the reviewer sub-agent to export WORKFLOW_PLUGIN_ARTIFACTS_DIR before running write-reviewer-findings.sh. The per-worktree-review-commit.md documents the fix (pass WORKFLOW_PLUGIN_ARTIFACTS_DIR env var to sub-agents so get_artifacts_dir() returns the orchestrator's ARTIFACTS_DIR), but the standard code-review-dispatch.md prompt template lacks this instruction.

get_artifacts_dir() in deps.sh already has the override hook (line 267): if WORKFLOW_PLUGIN_ARTIFACTS_DIR is set, it returns that path. The env var just needs to be passed to sub-agents.

Gate 1a: skipped — CLI_user tag not present but this is straightforward enough. Will dispatch basic investigation.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Classification: behavioral, Score: 2 (BASIC). Severity=1, Complexity=1, Environment=0. Bug is clear: get_artifacts_dir() hashes REPO_ROOT, and sub-agent worktree has different REPO_ROOT than orchestrator, causing reviewer-findings.json to land in different /tmp dir. Fix options: (1) --findings-file arg to record-review.sh, (2) set WORKFLOW_PLUGIN_ARTIFACTS_DIR in dispatch. Proceeding with BASIC investigation.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Fix implemented: Added --findings-file PATH flag to record-review.sh (lines 69, 88-95, 127-133). When a reviewer sub-agent runs in a worktree with a different REPO_ROOT hash, the orchestrator can now pass --findings-file to explicitly locate the reviewer-findings.json written by the sub-agent. Backward compatible: flag is optional, existing behavior preserved. Tests added: test_record_review_findings_file_flag and test_record_review_findings_file_flag_missing_path in tests/hooks/test-record-review.sh. REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md and per-worktree-review-commit.md updated with worktree isolation guidance. All gate checks pass (2a/2c/2d not triggered).
comments.author: Test
